rationale_for_baseline_tier: Benign but severe infantile seborrhoeic dermatitis with weeping — prompt prescription and skin-care advice with a clear safety-net for secondary infection and serious systemic illness in a young infant; not an emergency now.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Thick yellow pus, redness spreading across the neck/chest, fever, poor feeding, or lethargy
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Attend a paediatric emergency department urgently
escalation_edges.rationale: Secondary infection or a systemically unwell young infant needs urgent assessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the areas drain thick yellow pus, the redness spreads across the neck/chest, or your baby develops a fever, stops feeding or becomes floppy — go to a paediatric emergency department straight away.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Tiny blood-spots (petechiae)/bruising in the rash, or the baby is failing to thrive/systemically unwell
escalation_edges.rationale: Could indicate a serious underlying condition (e.g., LCH).
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you see tiny blood-spots or bruising in the rash, or your baby isn't feeding/growing well, get a prompt paediatric review.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Provide clear reassurance and a specific safety-net; support the parents.
Use only mild short-course topical therapy; lower threshold for review if unwell.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is severe cradle cap — harmless but it looks dramatic. Stop the oils, wash with baby shampoo and gently loosen the crusts, and use the mild cream on the red/weeping areas for up to 5 days. Watch for pus, spreading redness, fever, poor feeding, or blood-spots.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP within days for prescription/review; paediatric ED for secondary infection or an unwell infant
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: After-hours GP for the prescription; paediatric ED if the baby becomes febrile, feeds poorly or the rash becomes infected.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document infantile seborrhoeic dermatitis with intertriginous weeping (secondary infection/LCH screened negative), oils ceased, mild topical therapy, and a safety-net.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Self-care only with no secondary-infection/unwell-infant safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A secondary infection or systemically unwell young infant deteriorates before assessment.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine handling without stopping the oils or treating the flare.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance for benign cradle cap in a well, afebrile infant.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot palpate/assess for deep infection or systemic LCH features remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat as cradle cap (stop oils + mild topical + gentle care) and give a clear secondary-infection/systemic-illness safety-net with a low threshold for paediatric review.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Continuing oils, over-treating, or missing infection/serious disease.
